title: Sleepy no-op iteration leaks uncommitted Morty changes onto base branch
source_context: iter 1 BR-022 -> tester-morty -> no-op 7m36s; 0.4% budget used then idled all night
description: During a sleepy run on Benzineradar (ticket BR-022), the delegated Morty edited tracked files but never committed. Sleepy then ran 'git checkout base_branch', which carried the uncommitted changes from the sleepy/BR-022 branch onto the base branch (release-prep), and the empty sleepy branch was deleted. Result: unverified native code landed on the base working tree and the review queue was empty — the opposite of the 'isolated branch for review' guarantee.
proposed_changes: Before checkout back to base, either commit the Morty's work to the sleepy branch (even partial) or 'git stash'/'git checkout -- .' to discard. Never let a dirty tree cross a branch switch. Also: pick_next_ticket should not re-delegate the same todo ticket into a no-op idle loop, and agent_for_ticket chose tester-morty for a native implementation bug — should be fullstack/architect.
